text.title: Product Operations (PM-side) Core Rules
text.summary: PM-side RACI contract with an existing Product Ops function: consumes canonical artefacts, hands off scaled-org ceremonies, never duplicates instrumentation Product Ops owns.

rule.statement: The PM-↔-ProductOps boundary MUST be a written RACI per artefact (tracking-plan, OKR cascade, voice-of-customer, launch readiness).

rationale.source: Marty Cagan / Melissa Perri — Product Ops literature

rule.rationale: Without RACI, both sides build the same dashboard twice OR both wait for the other.

rule.statement: When Product Ops maintains a canonical store, the PM MUST consume from it rather than build a parallel one.
rule.rationale: Parallel stores fragment the truth and undermine Product Ops investment.

rule.statement: Once a PM-invented ceremony or template gains traction with >=3 PMs, it MUST be handed off to Product Ops for templating and scaling.
If Product Ops owns instrumentation, the PM MUST NOT add ad-hoc parallel instrumentation. Requests go through Product Ops backlog.
When PM-↔-ProductOps disagree, the escalation path MUST be named in the contract.
rule.rationale: Without a named path, the wrong exec gets dragged in and the relationship sours.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — no Product Ops function (use solo); <=2 PM team; consultancy with no recurring surface; recent RACI without org change), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
